---
title: "rnaVariantCalling"
author: "Chiara Folland"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/Users/00104561/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UWA/Research/Projects/AIM3_TRANSCRIPTOMICS/app/rnawebapp")
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
library(data.table)
library(vcfR)
```



```{r load, include=FALSE}
tsv<-"./data/new/MUSCLE_v113.VEP.pass.tsv"
rna_var<-as.data.frame(fread(tsv))

## Run bcftools and parse script on VCF

# Load required library for better system calls (optional)
if (!requireNamespace("processx", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("processx")
library(processx)

# Define directories and paths
rna_vcf <- "./data/new/MUSCLE_v113.VEP.pass.vcf.gz"
output_vcf <- "./data/new/MUSCLE_v113.VEP.pass.parsed.tsv"

```

```{rna_vcf_process, message=FALSE}
#' Process an RNA VCF File to Extract Annotations
#'
#' This function calls a Python script (`parse.py`) to parse a VCF or gzipped VCF file
#' and extract annotation fields into a tab-separated values (TSV) file. It supports both
#' compressed (`.vcf.gz`) and uncompressed (`.vcf`) VCF inputs.
#'
#' @param vcf Character. Path to the input VCF file (.vcf or .vcf.gz) containing
#' annotated variants (e.g., VEP annotations).
#' @param output_tsv Character. Path to the output TSV file that will store the parsed annotations.
#' @param parse_script Character. Path to the Python script used for parsing
#' (default: `"./parse.py"`). This script must accept input and output file paths
#' as arguments and produce a TSV file.
#'
#' @details
#' The Python script should already handle:
#' \itemize{
#'   \item Automatic detection of gzipped VCF files.
#'   \item Removal of columns that are empty across all rows.
#'   \item Parsing of INFO and CSQ fields to expand annotations.
#' }
#' 
#' This function constructs a system call of the form:
#' \preformatted{
#' python parse.py input.vcf.gz output.tsv
#' }
#' and executes it using \code{system()}.
#'
#' @return No return value. This function is called for its side effect of generating
#' a TSV file at the specified \code{output_tsv} location.
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' # Process a compressed VCF file and save output as TSV
#' process_rna_vcf(
#'   vcf = "MUSCLE_v113.VEP.pass.vcf.gz",
#'   output_tsv = "MUSCLE_v113.VEP.pass.tsv",
#'   parse_script = "/path/to/parse.py"
#' )
#' }
#'
#' @export
process_rna_vcf <- function(
    vcf,
    output_tsv,
    parse_script = "./parse.py") {
  
  cmd <- sprintf(
    "python %s %s %s",
    shQuote(parse_script), shQuote(vcf), shQuote(output_tsv)
  )
  system(cmd)
}

process_rna_vcf(rna_vcf, output_vcf)

```

```{r Rvcf, message=FALSE}


# Read VCF (this may still hit memory limits—see note below)
vcf <- read.vcfR("your_file.vcf.gz")  # Replace with your path

# Extract genotype matrix
gt <- extract.gt(vcf, element = "GT")  # matrix: variants x samples

# Count non-missing genotypes (i.e., variants) per sample
variant_counts <- colSums(!is.na(gt) & gt != "./.")

# Convert to data.frame for tabular display
variant_counts_df <- data.frame(
  Sample = names(variant_counts),
  VariantCount = as.integer(variant_counts)
)

# View or save
head(variant_counts_df)
```

```{r vcf, message=FALSE}
#' Filter RNA VCF for rare heterozygous or homozygous alternate variants
#'
#' Filters a VCF-like data frame to return only rows where the variant passed
#' rare filtering (`FILTER == "PASS_rare"`) and the given sample has a genotype
#' of `"0/1"` (heterozygous) or `"1/1"` (homozygous alternate).
#'
#' @param rna_vcf A data frame containing VCF-style variant data. Must include
#'   a `FILTER` column and a genotype column named after the sample ID.
#' @param sample_ID A string giving the name of the column containing the sample's genotype.
#'
#' @return A data frame of filtered variants for the specified sample.
#'
#' @examples
#' filtered <- filtered_VC(rna_vcf = my_vcf_data, sample_ID = "D21-0076")
#' head(filtered)
#'
#' @export
#'
#' @importFrom shiny req
#' @importFrom DT renderDT
#' @import data.table
filtered_VC <- function(rna_data, sample_id) {
  renderDT({
    req(sample_id())
    df<- rna_data[FILTER == "PASS_rare" & rna_data[[sample_id]] %in% c("0/1", "1/1")]
    util_nowrap_dt(df)
  })
}

filtered_VC(rna_data, "D14-1712")

```

```{r explore, message=FALSE}
#' ## Variant Calling Tables (first 1,000)

if (!all(is.na(rna_data$MAX_AF))) {
  DT::datatable(
    head(rna_data[FILTER == "PASS_rare" & `3804` %in% c("0/1", "1/1")], 1000),
    caption = 'Rare Variants called from RNA (up to 1,000 rows shown)',
    options=list(scrollX=TRUE),
    filter = 'top')
}

if (!all(is.na(rna_data$MAX_AF))) {
  DT::datatable(
    head(rna_data[FILTER == "PASS_rare"], 1000),
    caption = 'Rare Variants called from RNA (up to 1,000 rows shown)',
    options=list(scrollX=TRUE),
    filter = 'top')
}

# melt filters by GT. Exclude reference calls. Read in batches to avoid vector length errors for large datasets

# generate batch lists by config value: yieldSize
batches <- seq(0,nrow(res_plot),100000)
if(batches[length(batches)] < nrow(res_plot)) batches <- c(batches,nrow(res_plot))

# build dts by batches
out <- lapply(1:(length(batches)-1), function(i){
  melt(res_plot[(1 +batches[i]):batches[i+1]], #read through batches 1
       id.vars = "FILTER",value.name = "GT")[GT != "0/0",.N,by = c("FILTER","variable","GT")]
})

# combine batches and sum up the variables
res_plot <- rbindlist(out)[,.(N = sum(N)),by = c("FILTER","variable","GT")]

#' ## Table of variant calls by GT (first 1,000)
summary_dt <- dcast(res_plot, FILTER + GT ~ variable, value.var = "N")
DT::datatable(
  head(summary_dt,1000),
  caption = "Variant filters by GT", 
  options=list(scrollY=TRUE),
  filter = 'top')

#' ### Breakdown of variants by GT
ggplot(res_plot, aes(x = FILTER, y = N,col = GT)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_text(data = res_plot[,median(N),by=c("FILTER","GT")],
            mapping = aes(x=FILTER,y= V1,label = V1, vjust = -0.5),position = position_dodge(0.9),show.legend = F,size = 3.5) +
  ylab("Variants per sample") + scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(n.dodge = 2)) + theme_bw()

# Split res
res_plot[grepl("PASS",FILTER),FILTER := "PASS"]
res_plot[!grepl("PASS",FILTER),FILTER := "FILTERED OUT"]

res_plot_summary <- res_plot[,sum(N),by = .(FILTER,variable,GT)]

#' ### Plot only Pass/Fail split
ggplot(res_plot_summary, aes(x = FILTER, y = V1,col = GT)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_text(data = res_plot_summary[,median(V1),by=c("FILTER","GT")],
            mapping = aes(x=FILTER,y= V1,label = V1, vjust = -0.5),position = position_dodge(0.9),show.legend = F,size = 3.5) +
  ylab("Variants per sample") + scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(n.dodge = 2)) + theme_bw()

```
